title: Database Failover Delay — Postmortem
summary: Primary database failover took 22 minutes instead of the expected 5-minute RTO. Read replicas served stale data for 8 minutes after promotion. No data loss occurred; customer-facing write operations were queued and replayed successfully.

timeline.event: Primary DB health check failed; automated failover initiated
Replica promotion stalled on replication lag threshold
Write queue drained; services fully restored

root_cause: Failover automation blocked promotion when replication lag exceeded 30s, but lag metric was stale due to monitoring agent crash on the replica host.
what_went_well: Incident bridge formed within 4 minutes with clear roles
Write queue prevented data loss during failover window
Customer support used pre-approved status template within 10 minutes
what_went_wrong: Replication lag metric was not independently verified before blocking promotion
Runbook did not document manual override criteria
Stale-read detection alert fired 6 minutes after promotion

action_items.title: Add redundant lag probes on replica hosts
Document manual failover override in runbook

lessons_learned: Failover gates should fail open with human confirmation when metrics are ambiguous
Monitoring agent health must be part of failover readiness checks
Blameless review surfaced three quick wins without assigning individual fault
